flow_overview: A passenger facing rail travel website that lets users browse onboard travel information and reach dining menu resources through a hierarchical content flow.
capability_summary: Top level browsing of travel experience content
Hierarchical navigation through onboard amenities and services
Access to national and route specific café menu resources
Contextual side navigation and section wayfinding

requirements.text: The system shall expose major travel experience categories from the primary site navigation.
requirements.rationale: The home page shows an Experience entry in the main navigation, and the next screen expands it into multiple experience categories. This directly supports a navigable experience taxonomy.

requirements.text: The system shall allow users to navigate from general experience browsing into onboard travel information pages.
requirements.rationale: The ordered flow moves from the expanded Experience navigation to an Onboard page. The sequence strongly suggests navigation into onboard content, even though the click itself is not shown.

requirements.text: The system shall organize onboard information into distinct amenity and service categories so users can browse by topic.
requirements.rationale: The Onboard page groups content into areas such as accommodations, dining, baggage, WiFi, pets, and bicycles. This is a direct visible content organization feature.

requirements.text: The system shall provide dedicated detail pages for onboard dining information.
requirements.rationale: The flow shows an Onboard overview page followed by a specific Onboard Dining page with focused dining content. This supports dedicated dining detail views.

requirements.text: The system shall support drill down within onboard dining into specific dining service types such as traditional dining, flexible dining, café dining, and Acela dining.
requirements.rationale: The Onboard Dining page shows multiple dining subtypes, and the next screen lands on the Café subsection. The flow directly supports subtype navigation, though not every subtype is opened.

requirements.text: The system shall provide a dedicated café service page that explains the scope and availability of café service.
requirements.rationale: The Café page contains descriptive text about café service, meal and beverage availability, and general availability across routes and times. This is directly visible on the page.

requirements.text: The system shall present sample menu resources from the café service page.
requirements.rationale: The Café page contains a Sample Menus section with grouped links. This directly supports the availability of menu resources from that page.

requirements.text: The system shall provide access to a national café menu.
requirements.rationale: The Café page shows a National Café Menu link under the National grouping. The access point is visible, while the correctness of the linked destination is only partially verifiable from screenshots alone.

requirements.text: The system shall provide route or region specific café menu resources in addition to a national menu.
requirements.rationale: The Café page lists multiple regional or route specific menu links next to the national option. The links are visible, but their downstream contents are not opened in the sequence.

requirements.text: The system shall surface related dietary and food policy information alongside onboard dining content.
requirements.rationale: The Onboard Dining page includes sections for special dietary requirements, food facts, and personal food or beverages. The visible links support this capability, though their detailed content is not opened.

requirements.text: The system shall support list to detail navigation from an onboard overview page to specific amenity pages.
requirements.rationale: The flow begins with an overview style Onboard page and then moves into deeper dining pages. This suggests a browse and drill down pattern from overview content into focused detail views.

requirements.text: The system shall preserve contextual wayfinding by indicating the current section and subsection as users navigate deeper into onboard content.
requirements.rationale: Across the sequence, the left navigation persists and the active section changes from Onboard to Onboard Dining and then Café. This is a directly visible navigation feedback behavior.

requirements.text: The system shall present menu options for multiple onboard dining formats so travelers can distinguish among dining models before selecting a menu resource.
requirements.rationale: The Onboard Dining page names several dining formats and the Café page then exposes concrete menu resources. This supports the inference that the site helps users differentiate among dining models before following a specific menu path.

requirements.text: The system shall make onboard dining information discoverable through the public site navigation without requiring the user to enter a booking flow first.
requirements.rationale: The sequence starts from public navigation and reaches dining information pages through browsing rather than a purchase or itinerary workflow. That makes public discoverability a grounded hypothesis.

requirements.text: The system shall allow travelers to open the national café menu in a dedicated resource that can be viewed independently of the surrounding café information page.
requirements.rationale:
Contrastive import from staged bundle
Intended label: partially_fulfilled
Mutation family: external_effect_extension
Source gold requirement: REQ-08
Source gold text: The system shall provide access to a national café menu.
Generation rationale: This is a close extension of menu access into a downstream menu resource outcome. It stays tightly anchored to the visible menu-link capability while adding a realistic external-resource expectation that may only be partially settled from the flow.
requirements.parent_harvest_text: The system shall provide access to a national café menu.

requirements.text: The system shall make onboard dining menu resources reachable to public users without requiring sign-in or account ownership checks.
requirements.rationale:
Contrastive import from staged bundle
Intended label: partially_fulfilled
Mutation family: policy_or_role_extension
Source gold requirement: REQ-14
Source gold text: The system shall make onboard dining information discoverable through the public site navigation without requiring the user to enter a booking flow first.
Generation rationale: The gold set already supports public discoverability outside booking. Extending that to no sign-in is a realistic nearby policy constraint for public travel information, but it introduces account-state assumptions that the visible flow may not fully prove.
requirements.parent_harvest_text: The system shall make onboard dining information discoverable through the public site navigation without requiring the user to enter a booking flow first.

requirements.text: The system shall surface route-specific café menu resources only when they are applicable to the traveler's route or service context, while still preserving access to a general national menu.
requirements.rationale:
Contrastive import from staged block 6
Intended label: abstain
Mutation family: hidden_state_extension
Source gold requirement: REQ-09
Source gold text: The system shall provide route or region specific café menu resources in addition to a national menu.
Generation rationale: This keeps the same menu-resource feature space but adds a plausible applicability rule driven by hidden route context. The visible core remains close to the gold requirement, yet the rule itself is likely unresolvable from screenshots alone.
requirements.parent_harvest_text: The system shall provide route or region specific café menu resources in addition to a national menu.

requirements.text: The system shall provide corresponding detail or menu-entry resources for all dining service types presented from the onboard dining page.
requirements.rationale:
Contrastive import from staged block 6
Intended label: abstain
Mutation family: completeness_or_universal_quantifier
Source gold requirement: REQ-05
Source gold text: The system shall support drill down within onboard dining into specific dining service types such as traditional dining, flexible dining, café dining, and Acela dining.
Generation rationale: The gold requirement already supports subtype drill down. Strengthening it to all shown dining types is realistic and reviewable, but the ordered flow only demonstrates one subtype path, so later verification would likely need to abstain.
requirements.parent_harvest_text: The system shall support drill down within onboard dining into specific dining service types such as traditional dining, flexible dining, café dining, and Acela dining.

requirements.text: The system shall present an intermediate menu selection step that clearly separates national café menu access from route-specific menu access before the user opens a menu resource.
requirements.rationale:
Contrastive import from staged block 6
Intended label: not_fulfilled
Mutation family: missing_visible_step
Source gold requirement: REQ-09
Source gold text: The system shall provide route or region specific café menu resources in addition to a national menu.
Generation rationale: This is a disciplined workflow extension of the existing menu-resource branching capability. It adds a plausible explicit choice phase that is close to the same task space, yet such a separate step is often absent in compact informational pages.
requirements.parent_harvest_text: The system shall provide route or region specific café menu resources in addition to a national menu.

requirements.text: The system shall maintain consistent dining subtype naming and menu-resource terminology from the onboard dining overview through the café detail page so users can easily match the selected dining model to its menu links.
requirements.rationale:
Contrastive import from staged block 6
Intended label: not_fulfilled
Mutation family: cross_screen_consistency_extension
Source gold requirement: REQ-13
Source gold text: The system shall present menu options for multiple onboard dining formats so travelers can distinguish among dining models before selecting a menu resource.
Generation rationale: The gold item already relies on users distinguishing dining models across screens. Strengthening that into explicit terminology consistency is a realistic, UI-observable extension that could expose subtle mismatches across overview and detail states.
requirements.parent_harvest_text: The system shall present menu options for multiple onboard dining formats so travelers can distinguish among dining models before selecting a menu resource.
